description: Sensory perception, embodiment, words pointing at concrete entities in the physical world
judge_target: sensory grounding, words referring to concrete physical things, embodied perception
positive_prompts: I picked up the stone and felt its weight in my palm.
The barn smelled of hay and old wood.
Her hands were rough from years of working in the garden.
The doorknob was cold to the touch.
He could see his breath in the morning air.
The cat curled up against my leg, warm and heavy.
I tasted the bitter rind of the orange peel.
The hammer struck the nail with a flat metallic sound.
She brushed the flour from the wooden countertop.
The ladder creaked under his weight as he climbed.
Rain pattered against the tin roof above us.
The bread was still warm from the oven.
He wiped the sweat from his forehead with the back of his hand.
The water in the well was cold and slightly mineral.
She traced the grain of the table with her fingertip.
The old book smelled of leather and dust.
I held the lantern up to see further into the cave.
The horse's mane was tangled with burrs.
He heard the screen door slap shut behind him.
The lemon was firm and heavy for its size.
Her boots left prints in the wet sand.
The fire crackled and threw sparks toward the night sky.
I unwrapped the cheese and sliced it on the wooden board.
The river bank was muddy where the cattle had crossed.
He felt the rough bark of the oak as he leaned against it.
The kettle whistled and steam rose from its spout.
She picked the basil leaves from the plant on the windowsill.
The leather saddle was supple from years of use.
I could hear the cicadas in the trees behind the house.
The pavement was hot beneath my bare feet.
He carried the lumber across the yard one plank at a time.
The kitten's fur was soft against my cheek.
She held the candle close to read the inscription.
The fish jumped clear of the water and splashed back down.
I tightened the rope around the bundle of firewood.
The brass bell on the counter rang when the door opened.
He drove the wedge into the log with a heavy mallet.
The wool blanket was scratchy but warm.
She washed the pot in the deep stone sink.
The wind moved through the wheat field in slow waves.
I poured the milk into the pitcher on the table.
The dog dug at the dirt with both front paws.
He shouldered the rifle and started up the trail.
The leaves crunched under our boots as we walked.
She held the baby's foot in her hand and laughed.
The metal of the pipe was hot to the touch.
I peeled the bark away in long strips.
The owl's call carried across the valley at dusk.
He tied the boat to the cleat with a careful knot.
She kneaded the dough on a floured wooden board.
control_prompts: The concept was abstract and resisted easy definition.
Theoretical models accommodated the unusual data without modification.
Her thinking developed through years of disciplined study.
The principle was widely accepted in the relevant academic field.
He could articulate his position with characteristic clarity.
The argument curled back on itself in subtle and important ways.
I considered the bitterness of the moral compromise involved.
The proposition struck the committee as flat and unconvincing.
She brushed aside the objections raised by the skeptical reviewers.
The framework strained under the weight of the new evidence.
Criticism rained down on the policy after it was implemented.
The thesis was still warm in his mind from the recent defense.
He wiped away the doubts with a fresh round of analysis.
The reasoning was cold and slightly mineral in its precision.
She traced the development of the idea through the early letters.
The old theory smelled of an earlier era's prejudices.
I held the question up to see further into the implication.
The argument's structure was tangled with rhetorical flourishes.
He heard the criticism and dismissed it without reply.
The objection was firm and heavy for its modest framing.
Her arguments left traces in the journal's running discussion.
Debate crackled and threw off implications across the seminar.
I unwrapped the proposal and sliced it into manageable claims.
The argument's foundation was muddy where the data had been thin.
He felt the rough resistance of the established consensus.
The criticism whistled and warning rose from several quarters.
She picked the strongest examples from the dataset on her desk.
The supple framework adapted to years of expanded use.
I could hear the dissent in the back rows of the conference.
The pavement of conventional opinion was hot beneath the new claim.
He carried the analysis across the meeting one section at a time.
The rebuttal's tone was soft against my preconceptions.
She held the inference close to read its hidden assumption.
The objection jumped clear of the discussion and faded again.
I tightened the conclusion around the bundle of premises.
The brass note of authority on the panel rang when she spoke.
He drove the qualifier into the claim with a heavy footnote.
The proposal was scratchy but workable.
She washed the argument in the deep stone of historical context.
The debate moved through the proceedings in slow waves.
I poured the conclusion into the discussion at the table.
The premise dug at the assumption with both critical paws.
He shouldered the rebuttal and started up the methodology.
The objections crunched under our analysis as we walked through them.
She held the claim's footing in her hand and laughed.
The metal of the doctrine was hot to the touch.
I peeled the framing away in long conceptual strips.
The dissent's call carried across the discipline at dusk.
He tied the conclusion to the data with a careful inferential knot.
She kneaded the doctrine on a floured discursive board.
